about: Self-eval suite for pm-for-aidlc. gate_scenarios / routing_scenarios / stats_scenarios are auto-verified by run_evals.py against the real scripts; trigger_scenarios document should/should-not-fire expectations for manual or subagent testing. The negative gate + stats scenarios are regression guards for the hardened fixes (C1/C2/H1 round 1; C-A/H-A/M-1..M-5 round 2) — they MUST catch a reverted bug, not just pin current behavior. Round-2 guards: NEG-actuating-safety-low-blast-derived (C-A), gate-regression-nogo / gate-clean-go (H-A paired), judge-trusted-fixture (M-1), cost_calc-selftest, and held-out safety paraphrase + homograph routing (M-2/M-3).

gate_scenarios.desc: AT-56 read-only groundedness monitor (blast:low from bundle): autonomous+gated+safety:high stays unattended, NO canary.
AT-47 automated leakage gate: autonomous gated producer stays unattended with provenance.
AT-20 prompt regression CI: autonomous+gated+regr:high but blast:low -> unattended, eval-gate, NO canary/human_confirm.
AT-08 write eval rubric: human-led producer.
AT-03 thumbs-down triage: copilot partial-eval producer -> draft + human confirm + provenance.
AT-72 data-rights posture: human-led, eval:none, safety:high, producer.
The key inversion: autonomous but eval:none -> never unattended (blast given low to isolate the inversion).
C1 GUARD: an actuating (non read-only) autonomous safety:high task must NOT run unattended.
C1 GUARD: explicit irreversible actuation + safety:high never unattended even at blast:low.
C2 GUARD: blast omitted (unknown) must NOT bless an unattended run.
C-A GUARD: autonomous+gated+safety:high at blast:low with NO --actuation must NOT run unattended (actuation derives to reversible, not read-only — the carve-out must be asserted).
M3 GUARD: regr:high + eval:none must emit build_eval_first, NOT an unsatisfiable eval_gate.
P5 GUARD: external-high-stakes overlay sets gate_metric=pass^k and adds canary+safety_gate.
L-1 GUARD: safety:med must add safety_gate (axis is 3-valued, not binary) with NO human downgrade.

routing_scenarios.prompt: the eval score dropped after the last deploy, find the root cause
write the scoring function rubric for our summarizer capability
run a bake-off to choose a model on our eval set
monitor that every number in the output traces to a tool call
validate our llm judge against human labels and measure agreement
check whether training data leaked into the eval set
design a red-team campaign to find jailbreaks in the agent
plan a canary rollout for a model change gated on online scores
someone tricked the bot into ignoring its instructions
the model keeps leaking PII in responses
set up guardrails to block jailbreaks at runtime
write a PRD for our new AI feature
someone got the agent to reveal its hidden system prompt
routing_scenarios._note: M-3: held-out paraphrase NOT in the trigger list

routing_scenarios.prompt: users keep manipulating the model into saying offensive things
fix the memory leak in our inference server
routing_scenarios._note: M-2: homograph must NOT route to Safety

routing_scenarios.prompt: set up dependency injection in the agent codebase
routing_scenarios._note: M-2: homograph must NOT route to Safety

routing_scenarios.prompt: configure dependency injection across the system modules
routing_scenarios._note: M3-2: generic 'system' co-token must NOT route to Safety (held-out, not the literal guard string)

routing_scenarios.prompt: fix the file descriptor leak in the user service
routing_scenarios._note: M3-2: generic 'user' co-token must NOT route to Safety (held-out)

routing_scenarios.prompt: the model can be prompted to print its api keys
routing_scenarios._note: M3-6: secret exfiltration without the word 'leak'

routing_scenarios.prompt: the assistant revealed confidential customer records to another user
model is making things up in customer chat
the assistant keeps fabricating facts that aren't in the retrieved docs
routing_scenarios._note: PR2: held-out paraphrase for retrieval groundedness

routing_scenarios.prompt: our quality metrics tanked since Tuesday, figure out why
does our automated grader agree with human raters on tone checks
block merges if the new system prompt regresses our eval suite
responses feel different lately even though we didn't ship anything
evals look green but customers are complaining in prod
roll out the new model to 5% and roll back if scores dip
fix the css on the login button

stats_scenarios.desc: golden math checks incl. the pass^k(c>n) guard
M2: c>n must error, not return pass^k=2.0
H1: perfect point estimate but wide CI -> not TRUSTED
leakage gate exits 2 on a known (exact) dup
cost math golden checks incl. margin inversion
H-A: a paired regression (12 losses/0 gains) is NO-GO even though the bar passes
no regression vs identical baseline and bar passes -> GO
M-1: a strong judge at 100 balanced labels certifies TRUSTED on the full-set CI lower bound
M-4 GUARD: template-only overlap WARNs (exit 0), does not block a clean set
M-4 GUARD: --strict-template makes template hits block (exit 2)
H-A/M3-3 GUARD: paired-by-default compare flags a strict paired win (McNemar)
H-A GUARD: the unpaired test misses the same paired win (demonstrates the fix's value)
M3-1 GUARD: a candidate that drops baseline case_ids (coverage gap) is NO-GO even with no regression on the reported cases

trigger_scenarios.prompt: write an eval for our support agent's refusals
trigger_scenarios.reason: core eval-spec work on an AI system

trigger_scenarios.reason: trace triage + groundedness, no keyword needed
